title: Buy a Cheap Hyundai i10 UK | Common Issues & Buyer Tips | BuyAndScrap

content:
# Your Guide to Buying a Cheap Hyundai i10 UK

Looking for a reliable, economical, and nippy little car that won't break the bank? Then a Hyundai i10 could be right up your street! At BuyAndScrap, we know that finding a great car for a great price is top priority for many, and the i10 consistently delivers. If you’re on the hunt for a **cheap Hyundai i10 UK**, you've come to the right place for honest advice and top tips.

Often overlooked in favour of its flashier rivals, the Hyundai i10 is a true hidden gem in the used car market. It’s perfect for first-time drivers, city dwellers, or anyone needing a dependable second car. Let’s dive into why it’s such a smart choice and what you need to know before buying.

## Why Choose a Hyundai i10?

Before we get to the nitty-gritty of what to check, let’s quickly recap why the i10 is such a popular choice amongst savvy UK car buyers.

### Compact & Practical

From fitting into the tightest parking spots to zipping through busy urban streets, the i10 excels. Don’t let its small stature fool you, though; the interior is surprisingly spacious for its class, comfortably seating four adults and offering a decent boot for your weekly shop or a weekend bag.

### Economical to Run

One of the biggest draws of a Hyundai i10 is its low running costs. Expect excellent fuel efficiency from both the 1.0L and 1.2L petrol engines. Insurance groups are typically low, and road tax is often minimal, making it a very wallet-friendly option for daily commuting.

### Reliability You Can Count On

Hyundai has a strong reputation for building robust and dependable vehicles, and the i10 is no exception. While no car is entirely fault-free (more on that later!), the i10 generally enjoys high reliability ratings, meaning fewer unexpected trips to the garage and more peace of mind for you.

## What to Look For When Buying a Cheap Hyundai i10 UK

When you're searching for a **cheap Hyundai i10 UK**, it's crucial to be thorough. Even a great car can hide issues if you don’t know what to look for. Here are our top tips for inspecting a potential purchase:

### Service History is King

Always ask for a full-service history. A well-maintained car is a happy car, and evidence of regular servicing (even if it’s just basic oil changes) shows that the previous owner cared for the vehicle. This is especially important for older models.

### Rust? Check the Usual Suspects

While newer i10s are well protected, older models might show signs of rust, particularly around the wheel arches, sills, and under the boot lid. Give these areas a good visual check.

### Tyres & Brakes

These are easy wins. Check the tyre tread depth (1.6mm is the legal minimum, but aim for more), and look for even wear across all tyres. Uneven wear can indicate alignment issues. For brakes, listen for squealing or grinding during a test drive, and feel for any pulsing through the pedal.

### Interior Wear

Take a good look inside. While some wear and tear are expected in a used car, excessive damage to seats, dashboard, or controls might suggest a harder life. Check that all electricals (windows, radio, heating, wipers) are working correctly.

## Common Hyundai i10 Issues to Be Aware Of

No car is perfect, and being aware of common quirks can save you headaches down the line. Here are a few things to keep an eye (and ear) out for when checking a **cheap Hyundai i10 UK**.

### Clutch Wear

Particularly on older, higher mileage manual models, the clutch can start to feel a bit heavy or slip. During your test drive, pay attention to how the clutch feels when changing gears. Listen for any crunching noises.

### Suspension Noises

Some owners report minor knocking or creaking noises from the front suspension, often due to worn bushes or shock absorbers. Drive over some speed bumps or uneven roads to listen for these.

### Electrical Gremlins

While generally reliable, some older i10s can experience minor electrical issues, such as faulty central locking, window switches, or warning lights. Always test every button and switch you can find.

### Air Conditioning

Air conditioning systems are often neglected. Make sure the AC blows cold air quickly. Re-gassing can be costly, so it's best to check this before buying.

## Getting the Best Deal on Your Cheap Hyundai i10 UK

### Research Prices

Before you commit, do your homework! Browse BuyAndScrap.com to see what similar Hyundai i10 models (year, mileage, trim level) are selling for. This gives you a strong negotiating position.

### Haggling

Don't be afraid to haggle a little. If you've spotted any minor issues during your inspection, use them to try and bring the price down. Even a small reduction can make a difference.

### Test Drive Thoroughly

Never buy a car without a proper test drive. Take it on different road types – city streets, open roads, and even try reversing and parking. Listen for strange noises, feel how it handles, and check the brakes and steering responsiveness.

### Vehicle Checks

Always perform an HPI check to ensure the car isn't stolen, written off, or has outstanding finance. You can also check the car's MOT history online for free to see past advisories and failures.

## Is a Cheap Hyundai i10 Right for You?

For anyone in the market for a practical, economical, and surprisingly spacious small car that punches above its weight, a Hyundai i10 is an outstanding choice. It’s a fantastic everyday car, ideal for navigating British roads and keeping running costs low.

By following our tips, you’ll be well-equipped to find a fantastic deal on a reliable used i10. Happy hunting!
